steps.title: Identify the counterintuitive paradox
steps.content: Normally, price increases reduce demand. But here, comic book prices rose while demand surged ("buyers...suddenly thronged the market"). The blank must describe what the price rise caused.

steps.title: Apply the word meaning
steps.content:
The blank describes what the price rise did to demand. Substitute each word into "rising prices had the counterintuitive effect of ______ demand":

A. Monetizing means converting something into a source of revenue. The blank describes what happened to demand, not whether anyone profited — this word doesn't fit the causal structure.

B. Precipitating means to cause or trigger something suddenly. "Rising prices precipitating a surge in demand" captures the causal chain: the price increase triggered buyers to flood the market. ✓

C. Stabilizing means making something steady. The passage describes buyers suddenly thronging the market — a spike, not stability. This contradicts the passage.

D. Exploiting means taking unfair advantage of something. Rising prices didn't take advantage of demand; the buyers are the ones responding to opportunity — the direction of the action is wrong.

B is correct.
